notes: JMS v185 shares C++ mangled symbol names with GMS v95 for all character-domain functions. The login domain differs significantly (separate opcode space, different OnCheckPasswordResult wire layout). Character domain functions are present under the same demangled names.

calls.comment: update_time (get_update_time). JMS v185 PRESENT — same as GMS v95; atlas else-branch (no updateTime) is WRONG for JMS
nIdx (index). op-byte 0x14 personal (or 0x1F entrusted) consumed by dispatcher
ItemCRC (CItemInfo::GetItemCRC). PRESENT in JMS — atlas unconditional itemCRC fix is correct
nIdx (index). entrusted op-byte 0x1F consumed by dispatcher
count (m_asBlackList size). op-byte 0x1B consumed by dispatcher
per-entry name loop (string[]). JMS string[] — atlas unconditional []string fix is correct
nType (inventory type). op-byte 3 consumed by dispatcher
nMaplePoint (points). JMS reads only 2 ints — atlas else-branch (no prepaid) matches
mode (0x58 LOAD_LOCKER_DONE; op-byte consumed by OnCashItemResult dispatcher)
m_nBuyCharacterCount (*(this+290)). JMS PRESENT — atlas else-branch (2 shorts) is WRONG; JMS reads 4 shorts like GMS v95
mode (0x64 BUY_DONE; op-byte consumed by dispatcher)
isPoints bool (v47 == 2). op-byte 3 consumed by dispatcher
nCommSN (serialNumber). JMS NX-system: NO dwOption/currency int, NO trailing zero/oneADay/eventSN — diverges from all atlas branches
nCommSN (serialNumber). op-byte 0x21 consumed by dispatcher. JMS body = serialNumber only
commSN (serialNumber). op-byte 0x2E (NOT GMS 0x04). JMS gift = serialNumber ONLY — no SPW/birthday, no recipient name, no message, no oneADay. NX-system divergence
nCommSN (serialNumber). JMS has NO option int (atlas else-branch has option)
nCommSN (serialNumber). JMS has NO option int
spw (secondary password). op-byte 0x1B (NOT GMS 0x1C). JMS has SPW string (atlas else-branch has int birthday)
8-byte locker SN (Src). matches atlas unk long

CLogin::OnCheckPasswordResult.notes: JMS success path (resultCode==0 or 12) differs from GMS v95. Reads: Decode4(accountId)+Decode1(nGender)+Decode1(nGradeCode)+Decode1(combined)+2×DecodeStr(nexon club IDs)+5×Decode1(flags)+DecodeBuffer(8,chatUnblockDate)+DecodeStr(extra). Fundamentally different wire layout from GMS v95. Atlas login domain tracks this separately (task-027).

calls.comment: nGradeCode (packed byte, differs from GMS v95 Decode2)
nexon club ID 1 (JMS-specific: two strings instead of one)

CLogin::OnSelectWorldResult.notes: JMS success path similar to GMS v95 but reads m_bQuerySSNOnCreateNewCharacter before m_nSlotCount and m_nBuyCharCount. Loop is fixed at 18 iterations (v95 is count-based).

calls.comment: character entry loop (18 × GW_CharacterStat::Decode + AvatarLook::Decode + onFamily + rank)

CLogin::OnWorldInformation.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(nWorldID)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(state)+DecodeStr(eventDesc)+Decode2+Decode2+Decode1(blockCharCreation)+Decode1(nChannelCount)+loop channels+Decode2(nBalloonCount)+loop balloons.

CLogin::OnSelectCharacterResult.notes: Success path (resultCode==0 or 12, subMode==0 or 11) reads: Decode4(ip)+Decode2(port)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(authenCode)+Decode4(ulPremiumArgument). Same as GMS v95.

CLogin::OnCheckDuplicatedIDResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(resultCode).

CLogin::OnDeleteCharacterResult.notes: Wire layout: Decode4(dwCharacterID)+Decode1(resultCode). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: result code (0=success; error codes match GMS v95)

CLogin::OnCreateNewCharacterResult.notes: Success path reads GW_CharacterStat::Decode + AvatarLook::Decode. Same structure as GMS v95 success path. Error codes differ slightly (case -24 = JMS account notice). No trailing rank/viewAll bytes.

CUser::ShowItemUpgradeEffect.notes: JMS v185 reads Decode1×5: bSuccess+bCursed+bEnchantSkill+v5+v6. NO Decode4(nEnchantCategory). enchantResultFlag (v6) IS present in JMS — same as GMS v95 position. Only enchantCategory (Decode4, GMS>87 only) is absent from JMS. The || JMS clause on the enchantCategory gate was incorrect; the || JMS clause on enchantResultFlag gate is correct.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool::OnUserCommonPacket dispatcher before calling this function
bEnchantSkill (JMS: no nEnchantCategory Decode4 follows this)
v6 / enchantResultFlag (display flag — present in JMS v185 even though not passed to CUIEnchantDlg)

CUser::OnEmotion.notes: JMS v185 CUser::OnEmotion@0x9f636b reads Decode4(nEmotion)+Decode4(tDuration) only — no bByItemOption. JMS v185 is older code than GMS v95; the || JMS clause on the byItemOption gate was incorrect.

calls.comment: tDuration (display duration in ms — JMS has duration but NOT byItemOption)

CWvsContext::SendEmotionChange.notes: JMS v185 SendEmotionChange@0xb0b8be encodes ONLY Encode4(charId) from the local user (ms_pInstance+252). Does NOT encode emotionId, duration, or byItemOption. Fundamentally different wire format. Atlas server opcode 0x2B reads a single int4 which JMS client sends as charId; server reinterprets as emotionId. No duration/byItemOption for JMS.

calls.comment: characterId (local user's charId from CUserLocal — NOT emotionId)

CVecCtrlUser::EndUpdateActive.notes: JMS v185 EndUpdateActive@0xaaa076 encodes Encode1(detectFlag/bDetect). If detectFlag: Encode1(fieldKey from CField+328) + Encode4(crc from CField+756) + CMovePath::Flush. NO dr0/dr1/dr2/dr3/dwKey/crc32 fields. JMS movement is GMS v83-equivalent layout. The || JMS clause on all dr-field gates was incorrect.

CWvsContext::OnCharacterInfo.notes: JMS v185 OnCharacterInfo@0xb0aa6e INCLUDES monster book block (SomethingMonsterBook call at 0xb0ac66). The gate (GMS<=87 || JMS) in info.go is CORRECT for JMS. Decode sequence differs from GMS v95: reads Decode4+Decode4 for medals (not Decode1(medalSlot)), and reads monster book before MedalAchievementInfo::Decode.

calls.comment: v32 (medal-related int32 — JMS reads Decode4 not Decode1)
monster book block (SomethingMonsterBook — present in JMS v185)

CUserLocal::OnSitResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(sitting)+Decode2(chairId if sitting). Confirmed by IDA.

CWvsContext::OnChangeSkillRecordResult.notes: Wire layout: Decode1(exclRequestSent)+Decode2(count)+loop(Decode4+Decode4+Decode4+DecodeBuffer(8))+Decode1(sn). Same as GMS v95.

CWvsContext::SendStatChangeRequestByItem.notes: JMS v185 appends Encode4(timeGetTime()) at end vs GMS v95. JMS sends: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(0x1400)+Encode2(nHP)+Encode2(nMP)+Encode1(nType)+Encode4(extraTime). The trailing int4 is ignored by atlas server. Low-severity divergence.

CWvsContext::SendCharacterInfoRequest.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(dwCharacterID)+Encode1(bPetInfo).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnInit.notes: Wire layout same structure as GMS v95: Decode1(resetToDefault)+loop(Decode1+Decode4 per key). Loop count not determined from decompile; assumed same as v95 (90 entries). Same tool-limitation as v83/v87.

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnPetConsumeItemInit.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(m_nPetConsumeItemID).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnPetConsumeMPItemIni.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(m_nPetConsumeMPItemID).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::SaveFuncKeyMap.notes: Wire layout same as GMS v95: Encode4(mode)+loop of changed key entries.

calls.comment: count (number of changed key slot indices)

CUserRemote::OnAvatarModified.notes: JMS v185 uses list format for couple/friendship: Decode1(flags)+[if bit0: AvatarLook::Decode]+[if bit1: Decode1(speed)]+[if bit2: Decode1(carryItem)]+Decode1(bCouple)+[if: Decode4(count)+loop(DecodeBuf(0x10)+Decode4(pairCharId))]+Decode1(bFriendship)+similar+Decode1(bMarriage)+[if: Decode4+Decode4+Decode4]. GMS v95 had single-entry buffers for couple/friendship. Sub-struct difference, outer flags byte identical.

calls.comment: v3 flags byte: bit0=avatarLook, bit1=speed, bit2=carryItem
couple item SN (16 bytes per entry)
friendship item SN (16 bytes per entry)

CUser::OnADBoard.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(active)+[if active: DecodeStr(message)].

calls.comment: characterId (consumed by dispatcher before calling this function)

CUserLocal::OnBalloonMsg.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: DecodeStr(msg)+Decode2(width)+Decode2(duration)+Decode1(notAtPoint)+[if notAtPoint==0: Decode4(x)+Decode4(y)].

CUserLocal::OnSkillCooltimeSet.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(nSkillID)+Decode2(usRemainSec).

CWvsContext::SendAbilityUpRequest#Distri.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(dwFlag).

calls.comment: nCalcDamageIndex (controlled — atlas writes for JMS too)

CMobPool::OnMobChangeController.notes: JMS v185 production shape (function size 0x47 — same as v83, no dev seed block). Matches atlas's encoder.

calls.comment: bNotChangeAction (JMS: present per atlas `|| JMS` gate)
characterId — read by CUserPool::OnUserRemotePacket before dispatch

CMob::GenerateMovePath.notes: MOB_MOVE (opcode 0xC2). Atlas MovementRequest decoder mirrors this byte-for-byte. The four `>v83 || JMS`-gated blocks in atlas (multiTargetForBall + randTimeForAreaAttack, hackedCodeCRC, bChasing/hasTarget/bChasing2/bChasingHack/tChaseDuration) all fire for JMS — verified against this decompile.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(mode)-3 dispatch (0=Display/3, 1=SendSuccess/4, 2=SendError/5, 5=Refresh/special). mode=0 (Display): Decode1(count) + loop(sub_510E70) + new-memo popup if any. Same sub-op values as GMS v95.

calls.comment: memo entry decode loop (sub_510E70 per entry)

CMemoListDlg::SetRet.notes: JMS v185 CMemoListDlg::SetRet present at 0x6c2d43. Handles memo list display from CWvsContext::OnMemoResult Display sub-op. Wire structure: reads memo list from iPacket.

calls.comment: memo list entry (called per entry from OnMemoResult Display loop)

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#CapacityUpda.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnFriendResult@0xb2a873. Sub-op 0x14 (CapacityUpdate): Decode1(mode) switch; case 0x14: Decode4(buddyCount). case 0x15: Decode1(capacity). cases 7/10/18: CFriend::Reset. case 8: UpdateFriend. case 9: Invite = Decode4(friendId)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level). All sub-op values identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Invite.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 9 (Invite) in JMS OnFriendResult. Reads: Decode4(dwFriendID)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level)+CFriend::Insert(). Identical wire layout to GMS v95 case 9.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ListUpdate.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 7/10/18 (ListUpdate/Reset) in JMS OnFriendResult. Calls CFriend::Reset(iPacket). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: CFriend::Reset body — reads full buddy list from packet

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ChannelChang.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 0x14 (20) in JMS OnFriendResult handles ChannelChange. Reads Decode4(friendId) + Decode1(channel) + Decode4(map). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Error.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: error cases (11–15, 16, 17, 19, 22) in JMS OnFriendResult show string notices. Identical error sub-ops to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Update.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 8 (Update) in JMS OnFriendResult. Calls CFriend::UpdateFriend(iPacket, 1). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendSetFriendMsg@0x56e41d. Wire layout: Encode1(mode) + EncodeStr(name) + EncodeStr(group) + Encode1(flag). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendAcceptFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendAcceptFriendMsg@0x56e66c. Wire layout: Encode1(mode=0x1) + Encode4(friendId). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendDeleteFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendDeleteFriendMsg@0x56e5bd. Wire layout: Encode1(mode=0x2) + Encode4(friendId). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Add.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnPacket@0x8e447e dispatches mode 0 → OnEnter. Wire: Decode1(position)+AvatarLook::Decode+DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(channelId)+Decode1(pad). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Join.notes: JMS mode 1 → OnSelfEnterResult. Wire: Decode1(position). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Remove.notes: JMS mode 2 → OnLeave. Wire: Decode1(position). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#RequestInvite.notes: JMS mode 3 → OnInvite (static handler, no instance guard). Wire: DecodeStr(fromName)+Decode1(pad)+Decode4(messengerId)+Decode1(pad). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: mode byte = 3 (RequestInvite) — consumed by OnPacket before calling OnInvite

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteSent.notes: JMS mode 4 → OnInviteResult. Wire: DecodeStr(msg)+Decode1(success). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteDeclined.notes: JMS mode 5 → OnBlocked. Wire: DecodeStr(blockedUser)+Decode1(declineMode). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Chat.notes: JMS mode 6 → OnChat. Wire: DecodeStr(chatLine). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Update.notes: JMS mode 7 → OnAvatar. Wire: Decode1(position)+AvatarLook::Decode. No name/channelId/pad. Atlas Update wrongly writes name+channelId+pad — confirmed as wire bug in GMS v95 audit (GMS OnAvatar does NOT read those). JMS confirms same shape.

calls.comment: AvatarLook::Decode (no name/channelId/pad — atlas writes those incorrectly)

CUIMessenger::OnCreate.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnCreate (client-side handler for accepting invite). Sub-op 0 (ENTER): Encode1(0)+Encode4(messengerId). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnDestroy.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnDestroy. Sub-op 2 (LEAVE): Encode1(2). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg. Sub-op 3 (INVITE): Encode1(3)+EncodeStr(targetCharacter). Identical to GMS v95.

CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage.notes: JMS CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage@0x557267. Handles decline (sub-op 5): Encode1(5)+EncodeStr(fromName)+EncodeStr(myName)+Encode1(0). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::ProcessChat.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::ProcessChat. Sub-op 6 (CHAT): Encode1(6)+EncodeStr(chatLine). Identical to GMS v95.

CUser::OnChat.notes: JMS CUser::OnChat@0x9f5c74. Wire: Decode1(isGM)+DecodeStr(msg)+Decode1(bOnlyBalloon). Identical to GMS v95. characterId consumed by dispatcher before this function.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool dispatcher before OnChat

CField::SendChatMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendChatMsg@0x564a0a. Wire: Encode4(updateTime)+EncodeStr(text)+Encode1(bOnlyBalloon). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Created.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnPartyResult@0xb297e7 mode=1 (self-created). Wire: Decode1(mode)+Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(memberId)+Decode4(mapId)+Decode2(jobId)+Decode2(level). Same layout as GMS v83 OnPartyResult#Created@0xa3e31c. PARTYDATA (0x12A=298 bytes small) key finding: qmemcpy(v120, &this[1].m_Cookie.szReserved[584], 0x12Au). The v95plus gate in atlas member_data.go incorrectly included JMS — bug fixed in task-066 Task 10.

calls.comment: memberId (zeros ok — atlas writes EmptyMapId as placeholder)
mapId (zeros ok — atlas writes EmptyMapId as placeholder)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Invite.notes: JMS case 4 (Invite): Decode4(partyId)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level)+Decode1(autoJoin). JMS v185 DOES read jobId+level (same as GMS v87/v95). The v84plus gate in atlas invite.go (GMS > 83 || JMS) is correct.

calls.comment: jobId (present in JMS v185 — v84plus gate correct)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Join.notes: JMS case 7 (Join, mode=Join): Decode4(partyId)+PARTYDATA::Decode. PARTYDATA size = 0x12A=298 bytes. Case 8 (Join, mode=created): Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(charid)+Decode4(mapId)+Decode2(job)+Decode2(level)+memset(0x12A).

calls.comment: mode byte = 7 (Join with partydata) or 8 (Join newly created)
PARTYDATA::Decode (298 bytes — small format; JMS uses 0x12A not 0x17A)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Left.notes: JMS cases for party leave (mode ~10/11): Decode4+PARTYDATA (small, 298 bytes). JMS PARTYDATA confirmed 0x12A.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Update.notes: JMS case for party update (mode 33/36): Decode4(partyId)+PARTYDATA (small, 298 bytes). PARTYDATA confirmed 0x12A=298 by qmemcpy at function start.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Disband.notes: JMS Disband mode (mode=12, isForced=0). Same as GMS v95@0xa11085: Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(targetId)+Decode1(isForced). Atlas Disband writes mode+partyId+targetId+0x00+partyId — the trailing repeated partyId is atlas-only; pre-existing ❌ in GMS v83/v95 audits too.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Error.notes: JMS Error modes (mode=9/10/13/16/17/19/21/22/23/25/28/29/30/32/33/35/36). No further reads — error code only. Atlas Error writes mode+string(name), which is atlas-only extra field. Pre-existing ❌ in GMS v83/v95 audits too.

calls.comment: mode byte (error code — no further reads)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#ChangeLeader.notes: JMS ChangeLeader: mode byte + Decode4(newLeaderId) + Decode1(hasChanged). Same as GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#MemberHP.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnReceiveHP@0xa575be. Wire: Decode4(characterId)+Decode4(hp)+Decode4(maxHp). Same as GMS v95.

CField::SendCreateNewPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendCreateNewPartyMsg@0x56ca8b. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=1). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendWithdrawPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendWithdrawPartyMsg@0x56cba7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=2). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendKickPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendKickPartyMsg@0x56cf23. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=3)+EncodeStr(name). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendChangePartyBossMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendChangePartyBossMsg@0x56d0cc. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=5)+EncodeStr(name). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendJoinPartyMsg@0x56cce9. Wire for Invite sub-op: Encode1(sub-op=4)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg#OperationJoin.notes: JMS SendJoinPartyMsg OperationJoin sub-op: Encode1(sub-op=0)+Encode4(partyId)+Encode1(accepted). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#InviteReject.notes: JMS InviteReject serverbound (same opcode as party result, client sends): Encode1(mode=5)+Encode4(partyId)+Encode1(rejected). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnGuildResult@0xb22518. Full guild result dispatcher. Mode 5 (Invite) reads: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(inviterName)+Decode4(unknown)+Decode4(skillId) — same as GMS v87/v95. The v84plus gate (GMS > 83 || JMS) in atlas operation.go is correct. Mode values and wire layouts match GMS v95 for all audited cases. BBS-related modes (above 50) absent (CUIGuildBBS not present in JMS v185).

calls.comment: mode byte — dispatches to guild sub-handler

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#RequestAgreem.notes: JMS mode 3 (RequestAgreement): Decode4(partyId)+DecodeStr(leaderName)+DecodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Invite.notes: JMS mode 5 (Invite): Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(inviterName)+Decode4(unknown)+Decode4(skillId). v84plus gate (GMS > 83 || JMS) is correct for JMS — JMS reads the full invite fields.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessage.notes: JMS ErrorMessage: mode byte only → string notice. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Disband.notes: JMS Disband: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(msg). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberJoined.notes: JMS MemberJoined: Decode4(guildId) + full member entry decode. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberLeft.notes: JMS MemberLeft: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+DecodeStr(name)+mode-specific. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberStatusU.notes: JMS MemberStatusUpdate: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(channel)+Decode4(job)+Decode2(level). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberTitleUp.notes: JMS MemberTitleUpdate: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(title). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#EmblemChange.notes: JMS EmblemChange: Decode4(guildId)+emblem fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#NoticeChange.notes: JMS NoticeChange: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(notice). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#TitleChange.notes: JMS TitleChange: Decode4(guildId)+5×DecodeStr(titles). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#CapacityChang.notes: JMS CapacityChange (':' = 58): Decode4(guildId)+Decode1(newCapacity). If member of this guild and current member: display notice. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Info.notes: JMS Info ('9' = 57): guildId check + sub_510984 (guild data decode). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: sub_510984 guild data decode + qmemcpy 0x24 bytes

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#AgreementResp.notes: JMS AgreementResponse (mode 3 or 28): Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr+DecodeStr. Same as GMS v95.

CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged@0xa5763e. Wire: Decode4(charId, dispatcher prefix)+DecodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool dispatcher before OnGuildNameChanged

CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged@0xa57689. Wire: Decode4(charId, dispatcher prefix)+emblem fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CField::InputGuildName.notes: JMS CField::InputGuildName@0x56d98c. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=1)+EncodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendCreateGuildAgreeMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendCreateGuildAgreeMsg@0x56da47. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=3)+Encode1(agreed). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg@0x56e325. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=9)+Encode2(logoId)+Encode1(logoColor)+Encode2(bgId)+Encode1(bgColor). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendInviteGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendInviteGuildMsg@0x56dab9. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=5)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendWithdrawGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendWithdrawGuildMsg@0x56dcc7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=7). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendKickGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendKickGuildMsg@0x56ddf7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=8)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetGuildNoticeMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendSetGuildNoticeMsg@0x56e3a2. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=16)+EncodeStr(notice). Identical to GMS v95.

CTabGuildAlliance::OnGradeChange.notes: JMS CTabGuildAlliance::OnGradeChange@0x9ce1c7. Present in JMS v185. Wire: Decode1(grade). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket.notes: CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket and all CUIGuildBBS::* functions are absent from JMS v185. BBS (Bulletin Board System) feature was not implemented in JMS v185. This is a JMS-specific omission — out of scope for atlas wire fix (no JMS client to decode BBS packets). Affected: guild/clientbound/bbs_thread_list.go, guild/clientbound/bbs_thread.go, guild/serverbound/bbs_*.go.

CUIGuildBBS::SendLoadListRequest.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented. See CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket note.

CUIGuildBBS::SendViewEntryRequest.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented. See CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket note.

CUIGuildBBS::OnGuildBBSPacket#BBSThreadL.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnGuildBBSPacket#BBSThread.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnCommentDelete.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnRegister.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnComment.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnDelete.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CWvsContext::SendSetGuildTitleNames.notes: CWvsContext::SendSetGuildTitleNames absent from JMS v185. JMS uses a different title-names update mechanism. The serverbound title change uses CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg-family rather than a dedicated WvsContext function. Out of scope — no wire bug since atlas does not send this packet to JMS clients.

CWvsContext::SendGuildJoinMsg.notes: CWvsContext::SendGuildJoinMsg absent from JMS v185. JMS uses an inline or inlined join path. Out of scope — atlas does not send guild join packets to JMS clients in the current implementation.

CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage#DenyGuildRequ.notes: JMS CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage@0x557267 handles DenyGuildRequest (sub-op). Wire: Encode1(sub-op)+mode-specific fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberExpel.notes: JMS MemberExpel ('/') — same wire as MemberLeft but with expulsion flag. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ForeignNameCh.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged@0xa5763e. Same as OnGuildNameChanged entry above.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ForeignEmblem.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged@0xa57689. Same as OnGuildMarkChanged entry above.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessageW.notes: JMS ErrorMessageWithTarget: mode byte + DecodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CStage::OnSetField.notes: SET_FIELD (JMS v185 opcode 0x087/135). JMS CStage::OnSetField@0x7eea69, bCharacterData=1 branch. ENVELOPE-ONLY: CharacterData::Decode (line 165) is a single DecodeBuf boundary (character domain). JMS wire order: DecodeOpt(2 @line119 CClientOptMan::DecodeOpt) + channelId(4 @line121) + JMS-only byte(1 @line122, szCookie[76]) + JMS-only int(4 @line123, szReserved[1976]) + sNotifierMessage(1 @line126) + bCharacterData(1 @line127) + nNotifierCheck/notifierCount(2 @line128) + [if count: DecodeStr loop] + 3x damage seed(4 @lines159-161) + CharacterData + OnSetLogoutGiftConfig@0xae81c0 (Decode4 m_bPredictQuit + 3x Decode4 logoutGiftSN = 4 ints) + timestamp DecodeBuffer(p,8 @line209). Matches atlas set_field.go JMS branch field-for-field — JMS byte+int block, 4-byte nHP path N/A here (CharacterData path), 4 logout gift ints all confirmed.

calls.comment: JMS-only byte (@line122 → szCookie[76]) — atlas WriteByte(0) gated JMS
JMS-only int (@line123 → szReserved[1976]) — atlas WriteInt(0) gated JMS
CharacterData::Decode (@line165) — ENVELOPE BOUNDARY; inner shape under character domain
m_bPredictQuit (OnSetLogoutGiftConfig@0xae81c0) — logout gift int #1, gated (GMS>83 || JMS)

CStage::OnSetField#WarpToMap.notes: SET_FIELD warp path (JMS v185 opcode 0x087/135). Same JMS CStage::OnSetField@0x7eea69, bCharacterData=0 (else branch @lines168-190). JMS wire order: DecodeOpt(2) + channelId(4) + JMS byte(1) + JMS int(4) + sNotifierMessage(1) + bCharacterData(1=0) + nNotifierCheck(2) + revive(1 Decode1 @line170) + mapId(4 Decode4 @line181 / disasm @0x7eec6b) + portalId(1 Decode1 @line184 / @0x7eec87) + nHP(2 Decode2 @line187 / @0x7eec9d — 2 BYTES, NOT widened to Decode4 like GMS v95) + timestamp(8). NOTE: NO m_dwOldDriverID (GMS-only), NO chase byte (GMS-only). nHP is Decode2 — atlas FIXED to gate the 4-byte width on GMS>=95 only, JMS now writes 2 bytes (warp_to_map.go).

calls.comment: JMS-only byte — atlas WriteByte(0) gated JMS
JMS-only int — atlas WriteInt(0) gated JMS
revive flag (@line170; else branch) — atlas WriteByte(0) gated (GMS>28 || JMS)
nHP (@0x7eec9d Decode2 — 2 bytes; JMS did NOT widen with GMS v95) — atlas WriteShort gated (else branch)

CField::SendTransferFieldRequest.notes: FIELD_CHANGE / transfer-field request (JMS v185). JMS CField::SendTransferFieldRequest@0x56d75a: COutPacket(0x1D) then Encode1(fieldKey @line33, get_field()+328) + Encode4(dwTargetField @line34) + EncodeStr(sPortal @line40) + [if sPortal!=NULL: Encode2(x @line44)+Encode2(y @line46)] + Encode1(0 @line48, unused) + Encode1(bPremium @line49). NO chase byte, NO targetX/targetY int pair (GMS v95 has neither either — those are atlas's m.chase-gated tail). atlas change.go JMS path: chase gated GMS>=83 (skipped for JMS), targetX/Y only if m.chase (false) → JMS emits fieldKey+targetId+portal+[x,y if portal]+unused+premium. MATCHES.

calls.comment: sPortal / portalName (@line40; empty ZXString when NULL)

CField_ContiMove::OnContiState.notes: CONTI_STATE / field transport state (JMS v185). JMS CField_ContiMove::OnContiState@0x58e280 reads exactly two bytes: Decode1(v3=state @line5; switched 0/1/6 EnterShipMove, 2/5 LeaveShipMove, 3/4 AppearShip) + Decode1(v4=flag @line6; checked v4==1 in AppearShip). Matches atlas Transport: WriteByte(state) + WriteBool(overrideAppear).

calls.comment: overrideAppear flag (v4; checked v4==1 in AppearShip case)

CUserLocal::CheckPortal_Collision.notes: PORTAL_SCRIPT / script-portal collision request (JMS v185). JMS CUserLocal::CheckPortal_Collision@0xa0dde7 case 9 (v8==9, script portal): COutPacket(0x5F) then Encode1(fieldKey @line75, get_field()+328) + EncodeStr(portalName @line79, pt+4) + Encode2(x @line81) + Encode2(y @line83). Matches atlas portal Script (byte fieldKey + string portal + short x + short y).

CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaCreated.notes: SPAWN_MIST (JMS v185). JMS CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaCreated@0x436572 reads: Decode4(dwId @line103) + Decode4(nType @line104) + Decode4(dwOwnerId @line105) + Decode4(nSkillID @line106) + Decode1(nSLV @line107) + Decode2(phase @line108) + DecodeBuffer(16 rcArea RECT @line109) + Decode4(tEnd @line110). 8 fields — SAME as GMS v83 (no leading tStart). JMS does NOT have the v95-only tStart int. atlas affected_area_created.go (10-field v83-ish origin/lt/rb shape) matches NEITHER — STRUCTURAL DEFERRAL carried forward (see _pending.md AFFECTEDAREA-create-shape).

calls.comment: rcArea RECT (16 bytes = lt/rb as 4x int32 @line109)
tEnd (end time @line110) — NO leading tStart (v95-only; absent in JMS like v83)

CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaRemoved.notes: REMOVE_MIST (JMS v185). JMS CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaRemoved@0x436eda reads a single Decode4(v40 @line54) — the affected-area id. Matches atlas affected_area_removed.go (single WriteInt mistKey).

CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxEnterField.notes: KITE_SPAWN / message-box enter field (JMS v185). JMS CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxEnterField@0x6d5978 reads: Decode4(id @line67, v58) + Decode4(itemId @line78, +56) + DecodeStr(@line79, +8) + DecodeStr(@line85, +12) + Decode2(x @line92, +28) + Decode2(y @line94, +32). Field order: int id, int itemId, str, str, short, short — matches atlas kite_spawn.go (id,templateId,message,name,x,kiteType). String-pair semantic order (message vs name) does not affect wire shape.

CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxLeaveField.notes: KITE_DESTROY / message-box leave field (JMS v185). JMS CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxLeaveField@0x6d5f7f reads: Decode1(animationType @line48, v2) + Decode4(id @line50, v38). Matches atlas kite_destroy.go (WriteByte animationType + WriteInt id).

CMessageBoxPool::OnCreateFailed.notes: KITE_ERROR / message-box create-failed (JMS v185). JMS CMessageBoxPool::OnCreateFailed@0x6d594d reads NO packet bytes — just shows a StringPool notice (string id 0x200). Matches atlas kite_error.go (empty body).

CField::OnFieldEffect#Summon.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 0 / summon (JMS v185 opcode 0x087 dispatcher case via CField::OnFieldEffect@0x570359). Decode1(mode=0 dispatcher) then case 0: Decode1(effect v4 @line44) + Decode4(result @line45) + Decode4(v5 @line46). Maps atlas EffectSummon (mode+effect+x+y).

CField::OnFieldEffect#Tremble.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 1 / tremble (JMS v185, CField::OnFieldEffect@0x570359). case 1: Decode1(v18 bHeavyNShortTremble @line73) + Decode4(v19 delay @line74). Maps atlas EffectTremble (mode + bool + int).

CField::OnFieldEffect#String.notes: FIELD_EFFECT cases 2 (SetObjectState), 3 (ShowScreenEffect), 4 (play_field_sound), 6 (PlayBGM) — JMS v185 CField::OnFieldEffect@0x570359. Each: Decode1(mode) + DecodeStr(name/path). Maps atlas EffectString.

CField::OnFieldEffect#BossHp.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 5 / boss HP tag (JMS v185, CField::OnFieldEffect@0x570359). case 5: Decode4(v32 monsterId @line110) + Decode4(sName currentHp @line111) + Decode4(result maxHp @line112) + Decode1(v14 tagColor @line113) + Decode1(v15 tagBackgroundColor @line114). Maps atlas EffectBossHp.

CField::OnFieldEffect#RewardRullet.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 7 / reward roulette (JMS v185, CField::OnFieldEffect@0x570359). case 7: Decode4(v20 @line137) + Decode4(v21 @line138) + Decode4(v22 @line139). Maps atlas EffectRewardRullet (job/part/lev).

CField::OnBlowWeather.notes: BLOW_WEATHER (JMS v185 opcode 0x08B/139). JMS dispatch CField::OnPacket case 0x8B -> sub_5723E6@0x5723E6 (inlined, no OnBlowWeather symbol). JMS reads: Decode4(itemId v2 @line17) + [if get_consume_cash_item_type(itemId)==51: Decode4(v3 @line22)] + [if itemId!=0: DecodeStr(message @line27)]. NO leading blow-type byte (GMS OnBlowWeather reads Decode1 m_nBlowType FIRST). atlas effect_weather.go writes WriteBool(!active)+WriteInt(itemId)+[string if active] — leading byte is a JMS over-write AND atlas lacks the JMS cash-type-51 conditional int. STRUCTURAL per-version divergence on a BAD-FORM struct (no region branch) — DEFERRED to _pending.md WEATHER-jms-shape, not a blind fix.

calls.comment: itemId (v2 @line17) — JMS reads itemId FIRST; NO leading blow-type byte
extra int — ONLY if get_consume_cash_item_type(itemId)==51 (@line22; cash-weather variant); no atlas field

CField::OnClock.notes: CLOCK (JMS v185 opcode 0x090/144). JMS dispatch CField::OnPacket case 0x90 -> virtual call [vtable+0x2C] (CField::OnClock, inlined/unnamed in JMS185 IDB — no standalone symbol; OnDestroyClock@0x56ec69 confirms the clock family lives on CField). Clock is a BAD-FORM mode-switch struct (clockType byte + per-mode payload); same analyzer flattening limitation as GMS v95 (verdict carried forward as tool-limitation, not a wire bug). JMS clockType-discriminated shape matches the standard MapleStory clock layout; full per-mode decode not resolvable without the vtable target. ⚠️ manual-verify carried forward.

CNpc::OnMove.notes: NPC_ACTION (JMS185 opcode 0x119/281). Dispatched via CNpcPool::OnNpcPacket which Decode4(npcId) BEFORE CNpc::OnMove@0x7194d1 (dispatcher-prefix; atlas Action writes objectId as the first int). OnMove@0x7194e6 reads Decode1(nAction/oneTimeAction) + Decode1(nChatIdx) then (if has-move-path) CMovePath::OnMovePacket. SAME shape as GMS v95.

CNpcPool::OnNpcEnterField.notes: SPAWN_NPC (JMS185 opcode 0x116/278). CNpcPool::OnNpcEnterField@0x72068f reads Decode4(npcId @0x7206a1); the new-entry path then Decode4(templateId @0x7206d8) and CNpc::Init reads x/cy/moveAction/foothold/rx0/rx1. SAME shape as GMS v95.

CNpcPool::OnNpcChangeController.notes: SPAWN_NPC_REQUEST_CONTROLLER (JMS185 opcode 0x118/280). CNpcPool::OnNpcChangeController@0x720782 reads Decode1(localFlag @0x720794) + Decode4(npcId @0x720797); when localFlag!=0 it calls SetLocalNpc (new-entry path reads templateId + CNpc::Init fields). SAME shape as GMS v95.

CShopDlg::SetShopDlg.notes: OPEN_NPC_SHOP (JMS185 opcode 0x14A/330). CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7cb04e (nType==0x14A) -> CShopDlg::SetShopDlg@0x7c6536. Decode4(npcTemplateId @0x7c655a) + Decode2(count @0x7c6560) + per-commodity loop ('while ++idx < count', no max cap). Per item (@0x7c658d..0x7c661e): Decode4 itemId + Decode4 price + Decode4 tokenPrice + Decode4 itemPeriod + Decode4 levelLimited + (itemId/10000 in {207,233} ? DecodeBuffer(8) unitPrice(double) : Decode2 quantity) + Decode2 maxPerSlot. NOTE: JMS185 has NO discountRate byte and NO tokenItemId int that GMS v87/v95 inserted -- it is 5xDecode4 + qty/buffer + Decode2. Atlas ShopList gates DiscountRate (GMS>=87) and TokenTemplateId (GMS>=95) on Region==GMS; both are FALSE for JMS, so atlas emits exactly the JMS185 5-int layout -- MATCH. Analyzer flattens the loop -> loop-body rows are manually verified.

calls.comment: commodity count (loop bound; no max cap @0x7c6560)
[item] price / mesoPrice (@0x7c65a7) -- NO discountRate byte, NO tokenItemId int (JMS lacks the GMS>=87/>=95 fields)
[item] quantity (non-ammo arm; ammo itemId/10000 in {207,233} uses DecodeBuffer(8) unitPrice instead @0x7c6605/0x7c65f4)

CShopDlg::OnPacket#Simple.notes: CONFIRM_SHOP_TRANSACTION (JMS185 opcode 0x14B/331). CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7cb04e (nType==0x14B) switch(Decode1 mode @0x7cb0e5). Mode-only arms (cases 0,1,2,3,5,8,9,0xA,0xD,0x10,0x11,0x14 and default) read no further packet fields (StringPool Notice only). Atlas ShopOperationSimple writes only Byte(mode). MATCH (mode is the dispatcher discriminator).

CShopDlg::OnPacket#LevelRequirement.notes: Same CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7cb04e (nType==0x14B). Cases 0xE (@0x7cb1c0) and 0xF (@0x7cb23d) read Decode4 (level/count value formatted into an over/under level-requirement Notice). Atlas ShopOperationLevelRequirement writes Byte(mode) + Int(levelLimit). MATCH.

CShopDlg::OnPacket#GenericError.notes: Same CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7cb04e (nType==0x14B). NOTE: JMS185 has NO reason-string mode. Case 0x13 (@0x7cb0e5 arm 'case 4u/8u/0x13u') simply RETURNS with no further reads -- there is no Decode1(hasReason)/DecodeStr(reason) like GMS v95. Atlas ShopOperationGenericError writes Byte(mode)+Bool(hasReason)+optional AsciiString(reason); the discriminator/opcode is config-driven and the GMS shape is correct. For JMS the reason path is a client no-op (feature absent) -- OUT OF SCOPE for an atlas wire fix (template/config concern; GMS shape unchanged).

calls.comment: mode (generic-error discriminator; JMS case 0x13 is a no-op return -- NO hasReason/reason bytes)

CScriptMan::OnScriptMessage.notes: NPC_TALK wrapper (JMS185 opcode 0x149/329 via CScriptMan::OnPacket). CScriptMan::OnScriptMessage@0x7b7160 reads Decode1(nSpeakerTypeID @0x7b71a9) + Decode4(nSpeakerTemplateID @0x7b71b2) + Decode1(nMsgType @0x7b71bf) + Decode1(bParam @0x7b71c7), then switch(nMsgType). JMS185 dialog-type switch order: 0=Say,1=SayImage,2=AskYesNo,3=AskText,4=AskNumber,5=AskMenu,6=AskQuiz,7=AskSpeedQuiz,8/16=AskAvatar,9=AskPet,10=AskPetAll,12=AskYesNo(quest variant),13=AskBoxText,14=AskSlideMenu. SayImage=1 (NOT v83-shifted). NOTE: JMS185 has NO AskMembershopAvatar case (GMS v95 case 9 was AskMembershopAvatar; in JMS185 case 9 is AskPet). The secondaryNpcTemplateId (param&4) is read inside the per-type handler (OnSay) not the wrapper. Atlas NpcConversation writes speakerTypeId(byte)+speakerTemplateId(int)+msgType(byte)+param(byte)+[secondaryNpcTemplateId int if param&4]+detail. MATCH for the wrapper shape.

CScriptMan::OnSay#Say.notes: Dialog type 0 (Say). CScriptMan::OnSay@0x7b7315: (if bParam&4) Decode4(nSpeakerTemplateID override @0x7b7337) + DecodeStr(message @0x7b7340) + Decode1(bPrev @0x7b7356) + Decode1(bNext @0x7b7368). Atlas SayConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+Bool(Previous)+Bool(Next). MATCH (param&4 override int is handled by the wrapper's secondaryNpcTemplateId).

CScriptMan::OnSayImage#SayImage.notes: Dialog type 1 (SayImage). CScriptMan::OnSayImage@0x7b7496: Decode1(count @0x7b7524) + loop count x DecodeStr(image @0x7b753a). Count is a single byte (Decode1), matching atlas SayImageConversationDetail post-fix Byte(len)+loop AsciiString(image). FIX#2 re-check: SayImage byte-count MATCHES JMS185 -- no gate needed. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: image path -- loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens @0x7b753a)

CScriptMan::OnAskYesNo#AskYesNo.notes: Dialog type 2 (AskYesNo) and 12 (AskYesNo quest variant). CScriptMan::OnAskYesNo@0x7b765d: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7676) only. Atlas AskYesNoConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskText#AskText.notes: Dialog type 3 (AskText). CScriptMan::OnAskText@0x7b77bd: DecodeStr(message @0x7b77d8) + DecodeStr(default @0x7b77e8) + Decode2(nStrMin @0x7b77fd) + Decode2(nStrMax @0x7b780f). Atlas AskTextConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+AsciiString(Def)+Short(Min)+Short(Max). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskNumber#AskNumber.notes: Dialog type 4 (AskNumber). CScriptMan::OnAskNumber@0x7b7b0d: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7b28) + Decode4(default @0x7b7b3b) + Decode4(min @0x7b7b45) + Decode4(max @0x7b7b57). Atlas AskNumberConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+Int(Def)+Int(Min)+Int(Max). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskMenu#AskMenu.notes: Dialog type 5 (AskMenu). CScriptMan::OnAskMenu@0x7b7c95: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7cae) only (menu items are embedded in the message string). Atlas AskMenuConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskQuiz#AskQuiz.notes: Dialog type 6 (AskQuiz). CScriptMan::OnAskQuiz@0x7b84ef -> CWvsContext::OnInitialQuiz@0xb0e336: Decode1(flag @0xb0e34d); the flag==0 (show) arm @0xb0e394 reads DecodeStr(title) + DecodeStr(problem) + DecodeStr(hint) + Decode4(min @0xb0e3c3) + Decode4(max @0xb0e3cd) + Decode4(remainingTime @0xb0e3e1). Atlas AskQuizConversationDetail writes Bool(Fail)+[if !Fail: AsciiString(Title)+AsciiString(Problem)+AsciiString(Hint)+Int(Min)+Int(Max)+Int(TimeRemaining)]. MATCH (SAME as GMS v95). Guarded fields are collected unconditionally by the analyzer.

CScriptMan::OnAskSpeedQuiz#AskSpeedQuiz.notes: Dialog type 7 (AskSpeedQuiz). CScriptMan::OnAskSpeedQuiz@0x7b8501 -> CWvsContext::OnInitialSpeedQuiz@0xb0e493: Decode1(flag @0xb0e4a2); the flag==0 arm @0xb0e4ef reads Decode4(type) + Decode4(answer @0xb0e4f8) + Decode4(correct @0xb0e502) + Decode4(remain @0xb0e50c) + Decode4(remainingTime @0xb0e520). Atlas AskSpeedQuizConversationDetail writes Bool(Fail)+[if !Fail: Int(Type)+Int(Answer)+Int(Correct)+Int(Remain)+Int(TimeRemaining)]. MATCH (SAME as GMS v95).

CScriptMan::OnAskAvatar#AskAvatar.notes: Dialog type 8/16 (AskAvatar). CScriptMan::OnAskAvatar@0x7b7e1d: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7e3e) + Decode1(count @0x7b7e52) + loop count x Decode4(style @0x7b7e6e). Atlas AskAvatarConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+loop Int(style). MATCH. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: style id -- loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens @0x7b7e6e)

CScriptMan::OnAskPet#AskPet.notes: Dialog type 9 (AskPet) -- NOTE: in GMS v95 case 9 was AskMembershopAvatar; JMS185 maps case 9 to AskPet (AskMembershopAvatar is ABSENT in JMS185). CScriptMan::OnAskPet@0x7b7fc2: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7fde) + Decode1(count @0x7b7ff8) + loop count x [DecodeBuffer(8) cashId @0x7b8046 + Decode1(unused @0x7b804e)]. Atlas AskPetConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+loop[Long(cashId)+Byte(0)]. MATCH. Analyzer flattens the loop.

CScriptMan::OnAskPetAll#AskPetAll.notes: Dialog type 10 (AskPetAll). CScriptMan::OnAskPetAll@0x7b8250: DecodeStr(message @0x7b826a) + Decode1(count @0x7b8281) + Decode1(exceptionExists @0x7b8290) + loop count x [DecodeBuffer(8) cashId @0x7b82d7 + Decode1(unused @0x7b82df)]. Atlas AskPetAllConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+Bool(ExceptionExists)+loop[Long(cashId)+Byte(0)]. MATCH. Analyzer flattens the loop.

CScriptMan::OnAskBoxText#AskBoxText.notes: Dialog type 13 (AskBoxText). CScriptMan::OnAskBoxText@0x7b7966: DecodeStr(message @0x7b7981) + DecodeStr(default @0x7b7991) + Decode2(col @0x7b79a6) + Decode2(line @0x7b79b8). Atlas AskBoxTextConversationDetail writes AsciiString(Message)+AsciiString(Def)+Short(Col)+Short(Line). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskSlideMenu#AskSlideMenu.notes: Dialog type 14 (AskSlideMenu). CScriptMan::OnAskSlideMenu@0x7b8513 -> sub_7E2A97@0x7e2a97 reads Decode4(slideDlgType / this[37] @0x7e2ab4) + Decode4(menuType / v13 @0x7e2ac2) + DecodeStr(message @0x7e2ac9). JMS185 reads BOTH leading ints UNCONDITIONALLY (2x Decode4 + string). Atlas AskSlideMenuConversationDetail gates the FIRST int (Unknown 0/1) on Region==GMS && major>83 and writes only Int(MenuType)+AsciiString(Message) for JMS -- i.e. atlas writes 1 int + string for JMS but JMS185 expects 2 ints + string. POTENTIAL JMS GAP: the leading slideDlgType int is omitted for JMS by the GMS>83 gate. This matches the existing carry-forward manual-verify status for AskSlideMenu; documented as a concern. (Whether JMS templates actually wire AskSlideMenu determines runtime impact; the GMS shape is correct and the gate is GMS-major-driven.)

calls.comment: slideDlgType (this[37] @0x7e2ab4) -- JMS reads this unconditionally; atlas gates it GMS>83

CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg#Message.notes: TALK_GUIDE / tutor message. CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg@0xa2d342 reads Decode1(bByMessage @0xa2d35f); the FALSE (==0) arm @0xa2d38d calls DecodeStr(message) + Decode4(width) + Decode4(duration) -> CTutor::OnMessage(ZXString,int,int). FIX#1 re-check: JMS185 has the SAME tutor split as GMS v95 -- leading bool FALSE selects the string/message arm. Atlas GuideTalkMessage encodes the leading bool as false. MATCH -- no gate needed.

CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg#Idx.notes: TALK_GUIDE / tutor hint-index. Same CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg@0xa2d342; the TRUE (!=0) arm @0xa2d371 calls Decode4(hintId) + Decode4(duration) -> CTutor::OnMessage(int,int), NO string. FIX#1 re-check: JMS185 matches GMS v95 -- leading bool TRUE selects the hint-index arm. Atlas GuideTalkIdx encodes the leading bool as true. MATCH -- no gate needed.

CUserLocal::TalkToNpc.notes: NPC_TALK (JMS185 opcode 0x32/50). CUserLocal::TalkToNpc@0xa2cc90 (non-quest branch): COutPacket(0x32 @0xa2cd00) + Encode4(npcId / m_dwNpcId @0xa2cd15) + Encode2(GetPos.x @0xa2cd30) + Encode2(GetPos.y @0xa2cd48). Atlas StartConversation writes Int(npcId)+Short(x)+Short(y). MATCH.

CNpc::GenerateMovePath.notes: NPC_ACTION (JMS185 opcode 0xD0/208). CNpc::GenerateMovePath@0x7199ce: COutPacket(0xD0 @0x719a6d) + Encode4(m_dwNpcId @0x719a7e) + Encode1(nAction @0x719a89) + Encode1(nChatIdx @0x719a94) then (if has-move-path) CMovePath::Flush. Atlas serverbound Action writes Int(npcId)+Byte(action)+Byte(chatIdx)+movePath. MATCH.

CShopDlg::OnPacket#ShopDispatch.notes: NPC_SHOP (JMS185 opcode 0x35/53) op-byte dispatcher. The shop senders (CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest@0x7ca2c9, SendSellRequest@0x7cacab, SendRechargeRequest@0x7caecf) each build COutPacket(53) with a leading Encode1(op) discriminator (0=buy,1=sell,2=recharge). Atlas serverbound Shop wraps the op byte + per-op body. MATCH (op byte is the discriminator).

CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (JMS185 opcode 0x35/53) BUY body (op=0). CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest@0x7ca2c9: COutPacket(53 @0x7caae1) + Encode1(0 @0x7caaf3) + Encode2(slot/commodity index @0x7cab10) + Encode4(itemId @0x7cab20) + Encode2(quantity @0x7cab2b). Atlas ShopBuy writes Byte(0)+Short(slot)+Int(itemId)+Short(quantity). MATCH.

CShopDlg::SendSellRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (JMS185 opcode 0x35/53) SELL body (op=1). CShopDlg::SendSellRequest@0x7cacab: COutPacket(53 @0x7cae58) + Encode1(1 @0x7cae68) + Encode2(nPOS/slot @0x7cae73) + Encode4(nItemID @0x7cae7e) + Encode2(quantity @0x7cae89). Atlas ShopSell writes Byte(1)+Short(slot)+Int(itemId)+Short(quantity). MATCH.

CShopDlg::SendRechargeRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (JMS185 opcode 0x35/53) RECHARGE body (op=2). CShopDlg::SendRechargeRequest@0x7caecf: COutPacket(53 @0x7cafea) + Encode1(2 @0x7caff8) + Encode2(nPos/slot @0x7cb001). Atlas ShopRecharge writes Byte(2)+Short(slot). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnSay#Reply.notes: NPC_TALK_MORE (JMS185 opcode 0x33/51) generic continue reply (Say). Built inside CScriptMan::OnSay@0x7b7315 after DoModal: COutPacket(0x34 @0x7b7418) + Encode1(0 = msgType SAY @0x7b7425) + Encode1(action @0x7b7447). No trailing body for SAY. Atlas ContinueConversation writes Byte(msgType)+Byte(action). MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskMenu#Selection.notes: NPC_TALK_MORE (JMS185 opcode 0x33/51) menu-selection reply trailing field. Built inside CScriptMan::OnAskMenu@0x7b7c95 after DoModal: COutPacket(0x34 @0x7b7d74) + Encode1(5 = msgType AskMenu @0x7b7d82) + Encode1(action @0x7b7da4/0x7b7d98) + (if action==1: Encode4 selection @0x7b7daf). Atlas ContinueConversationSelection writes Byte(msgType)+Byte(action)+[Int(selection) if action==1]. MATCH.

CScriptMan::OnAskText#Reply.notes: NPC_TALK_MORE (JMS185 opcode 0x33/51) text-answer reply trailing field. Built inside CScriptMan::OnAskText@0x7b77bd after DoModal: COutPacket(0x34 @0x7b78c6) + Encode1(3 = msgType AskText @0x7b78d4) + Encode1(action @0x7b78e2/0x7b790b) + (if action==1: EncodeStr answer @0x7b7900). Atlas ContinueConversationText writes Byte(msgType)+Byte(action)+[AsciiString(answer) if action==1]. MATCH.

calls.comment: nHP (JMS v185 int16 — NOT widened to int32 unlike GMS v95)
IP address as raw uint32 (stored into pAddr.sin_addr.S_un.S_addr)
port (host byte order; sub_4AFC56 builds sockaddr)

CUserLocal::OnSetDirectionMode.note: JMS v185 uses sub_A2CD83 at case 0xE7 of CUserLocal::OnPacket instead of a named OnSetDirectionMode. Reads ONLY Decode1(bSet) — no tAfterLeaveDirectionMode int32 field. Same wire shape as GMS v83; gate GMS && MajorVersion>=90 is CORRECT for JMS.

calls.comment: nPOP (new total fame — CUIUserInfo::NotifyGivePopResult uses Decode4)
mode (cases 1-4 = error codes — no additional fields)
mode (case 7 = OPEN_SHOP; calls SendOpenShopRequest — no further reads)
mode (cases 9/10/11/15 — string pool notice, no further reads; JMS adds case 0xB/0xF vs GMS 9/10/15)
success flag (if 0 shows fail msg; if 1 shows success)

CWvsContext::OnEntrustedShopCheckResult#.note: Mode 0x12 (FreeFormNotice) is ABSENT from JMS v185 switch — falls to default/return. JMS does not implement this mode. Atlas FreeFormNotice encoder writes mode 0x12 which JMS client ignores.

CClientSocket::OnConnect#Hello.note: JMS v185 parses Hello via raw socket recv loop + CIOBufferManipulator::DecodeStr, not CInPacket. Wire format: 2B length + 2B majorVersion(uint16) + DecodeStr(minorVersion) + 4B recvIv + 4B sendIv + 1B locale. Locale must be 3 for JMS (checked at 0x4b02c6). Same overall wire layout as GMS.

calls.comment: dwCharacterId (opcode 0x5A; taken from CUser object at offset +4992)
action type byte (literal 3u for resign; opcode 0x66)

CQuest::StartQuest#ActionScriptStart.note: JMS v185 CQuest::StartQuest action=4 (m_nState==0, IsStartScriptLinkedQuest). Sub-struct after Action header (action byte + questId): npcId+x+y. No extra nItemPos field in JMS for this action. Same as GMS v95.

CQuest::StartQuest#ActionScriptEnd.note: JMS v185 CQuest::StartQuest action=5 (m_nState==1, IsCompleteScriptLinkedQuest). Sub-struct after Action header (action byte + questId): npcId+x+y. Same as GMS v95.

CLogin::OnCheckPinCodeResult#RegisterPin.note: CLogin::OnCheckPinCodeResult absent from JMS v185 IDB. JMS usesPin=false per template_jms_185_1.json; no pin flow exists. No JMS audit entry generated.

CLogin::SendSetGenderPacket.note: CLogin::SendSetGenderPacket absent from JMS v185 IDB. JMS login flow differs from GMS; gender-setting packet not found in this binary.

CClientSocket::OnConnect#ChannelConnect.note: JMS v185 CClientSocket::OnConnect non-login branch (0x4b051f): sends Encode4(charId)+EncodeBuffer(machineId,16)+Encode2(dummy1)+Encode1(0)+EncodeBuffer(unknown2,8). The gm/dummy1 field is Encode2 (uint16) in JMS vs Encode1 (byte) in GMS. GATE WIDENED: ChannelConnect.Encode/Decode now uses ReadUint16/WriteShort for Region==JMS.

calls.comment: gm/dummy1 uint16 (CConfig::dummy1) — JMS sends 2 bytes; GMS sends 1 byte

CClientSocket::OnConnect#StartError.note: JMS v185 login branch (0x4b03d1): COutPacket(v47, 15) + Encode2(v22, len) + EncodeBuffer(Src, len). Wire layout matches GMS v95 StartError: Encode2(length) + EncodeBuffer(bytes, length).

calls.comment: length uint16 (byte count of exception log data)
